name: Perciatelli with Fresh Sardines
recipeIngredient: 1 medium red onion, chopped into 1/2-inch dice
1/4 cup currants, soaked for 15 minutes in warm water and drained
2 bunches Italian parsley, finely chopped, to yield 1/2 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts of water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons of salt.
2. Scale, gut, and fillet the sardines (or have a fishmonger do this for you).
3. In a 12- to 14-inch saute pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat until just smoking.
4. Add onion, currants, pine nuts, anchovies, and red pepper; cook until softened, about 8 to 10 minutes.
5. Add sardine fillets and fennel leaves; continue cooking for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring carefully, until the fish pieces are just cooked.
6. Cook pasta according to package instructions until just al dente; drain well.
7. Toss hot pasta into the pan with sardines and stir to coat.
8. Add 1/4 cup toasted bread crumbs and parsley; stir through and serve immediately.
9. Sprinkle remaining bread crumbs on top before serving.
Yield: 4 servings
